user.title: MQTT Broker & Features
user.description: Configure the MQTT broker connection and optional features. If unsure about features, the defaults work well for most setups.

data_description.discovery_enabled: When enabled, publishes auto-discovery messages so other MQTT clients (like a second Home Assistant) can automatically find your devices.
data_description.discovery_prefix: The MQTT topic prefix used for discovery messages. Only change this if your other MQTT clients expect a different prefix. Default: homeassistant.
data_description.retain: When enabled, the MQTT broker remembers the last value for each topic. This means new subscribers immediately get the current state instead of waiting for the next change. Recommended: keep enabled.
data_description.qos:
Quality of Service controls message delivery reliability.

0 = At most once (fire and forget) - Fastest, messages may be lost. Best for frequent sensor updates where missing one reading doesn't matter.

1 = At least once (acknowledged) - Messages are guaranteed to arrive but may be delivered more than once. Good balance for most smart home use.

2 = Exactly once (guaranteed) - Slowest but most reliable. Every message arrives exactly once. Only needed for critical actions like door locks.

Recommended: 0 for most setups.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.description: Select which integrations to expose via MQTT.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to MQTT broker. Check host, port, and credentials.
